name: Durable Agent Recovery Validation
description: Validate durable child-agent recovery against an execution-scoped OpenShell workspace after a mid-run durable-agent restart.

document.name: durable-agent-recovery-validation
document.title: Durable Agent Recovery Validation
document.summary: Provision an execution-scoped OpenShell workspace, run one long-lived durable child agent step, restart the durable-agent pod mid-run, and verify the same workspace resumes cleanly.

input.promptPlaceholder: Leave the default unless you are intentionally changing the recovery validation scenario.
input.defaultPrompt: Validate durable child-agent recovery against the same OpenShell workspace after a durable-agent pod restart.

sleepSeconds.label: Sleep Duration
sleepSeconds.description: How long the in-sandbox command should sleep before finishing. This gives time to restart durable-agent mid-run.

maxTurns.label: Max Turns
maxTurns.description: Iteration budget for the durable child agent. The workflow is designed to finish in a single tool turn plus completion.

with.prompt:
You are validating Dapr durable child-agent recovery inside a parent SW 1.0 workflow.

User task:
{{trigger.prompt}}

Execution rules:
- Use the execute_command tool exactly once.
- Work only inside the current workspace.
- Run one shell command that does all of the following in order:
  1. prints the current working directory,
  2. creates validation-output,
  3. writes validation-output/pre-restart.txt with exactly PRE_RESTART_READY,
  4. sleeps for {{trigger.sleepSeconds}} seconds,
  5. writes validation-output/post-restart.txt with exactly POST_RESTART_CONFIRMED,
  6. prints both files and a directory listing for validation-output.
- After the command succeeds, stop immediately.
- End with a concise summary that includes the exact phrase RECOVERY VALIDATED.
- Do not use network commands.
- Do not ask for confirmation.
with.stopCondition: Stop only after validation-output/pre-restart.txt and validation-output/post-restart.txt both exist and the final answer includes the exact phrase RECOVERY VALIDATED.

doneTool.name: done
doneTool.description: Signal completion after the recovery validation command has finished successfully.

prepareStep.appendInstructions: Use execute_command exactly once. After the command succeeds, do not call any more executable tools. Call the done tool with answer set to RECOVERY VALIDATED.

agentConfig.name: durable-recovery-validation
agentConfig.instructions: Use execute_command exactly once to create pre-restart and post-restart markers in validation-output, verify them, and then finish with RECOVERY VALIDATED.
